subject: Remove tags for ELK healthcheck
commit_message:
Remove tags for ELK healthcheck

Elasticsearch, Logstash and Kibana tests are only relevant for OOM
For heat based installation it leads to errors (ELK not installed)
Until the installation still officially supports OOM and Heat, it makes sense
to exclude tests that are not supported by one of the installers from the health
or core list.

This patch shall be cherry-picked in Beijing
Core and health tags could be added again if
  - OOM becomes the only official installer
  - Heat installer supports ELK

It is possible also to use the --exclude oom option
but as it is persistent, it is as logical to remove the tags

Issue-ID: TEST-102

Change-Id: Iee97aa417f04978c222b882a5f2b07ed777f8a0b
Signed-off-by: mrichomme <morgan.richomme@orange.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

useful to avoid confusion when using Heat installation

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

please update the installer to exclude the heat tag in oom deployments and heat tag in oom deployments. This will allow you to have customized health checks while still clearly labeling them as health (so you can see them under the health tag in the results

Patch Set 1:

my comment should say heat tag in oom deployments and oom tag in heat deployments :)

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

ete.sh already excludes the oom test cases

Patch Set 1:

> my comment should say heat tag in oom deployments and oom tag in
 > heat deployments :)

ok so it is assumed that there is an explicit dependency between the installer and the tests, right?

the procedure is : the installer must clean the tags before running tests accordingly (heat => removes oom tag and oom removes heat tag)

and not

all the tests from the health must run whatever ONAP installation method
and additional oom tests could be run from oom installer / heat tests to be done from heat installer

Do I understand correctly?

If so I assume, I can abandon the patch

Patch Set 1:

its not clean, its just add --exclude oom or --exclude heat and robot will do the work for you and not even run or show them
